chunks.text: At sunset, I walked alone to a small lake near my house.
The sky was orange and purple, and in the distance, a thin moon gently appeared.
Beside the lake, a big branch of a tree was quietly pointing toward the water.
A single leaf, fallen from the tree, floated slowly on the water.
On the quiet surface without a wave, my shadow was reflected.
When a little wind blew, the water reflected the light.
The first star began to show faintly, and in the eastern sky, one by one
When I looked up, a white cloud was flowing like cotton.
In winter mornings, the surface of this lake is covered with thin ice.
It is not as wide as the ocean
but this little lake is a treasure for our town.
